title: Just one Mass. college ranks among the nation’s best graduate schools, per U.S. News
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: MIT ranked No. 1 for best graduate engineering program in the U.S. News 2026 rankings.
No Massachusetts medical schools made the top tier in medical research or primary care.
Northeastern University and UMass Boston graduate nursing programs dropped significantly in the rankings.

title: Suffolk DA Kevin Hayden appears to have a new primary challenger. She used to work in his office.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Linda Champion, a former member of DA Kevin Hayden's team, filed paperwork for a potential primary challenge.
Champion previously ran for Suffolk DA in 2018, placing fifth in the Democratic primary won by Rachael Rollins.
Kevin Hayden faces scrutiny for charging a Boston police officer with manslaughter, amid reelection campaign developments.

title: Malden cook goes viral for selling trendy Ilocos empanadas out of her apartment
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Janz Van's Ilocos empanadas gained popularity online, leading to Malden's Board of Health closing her home-based operation.
Van, with a background in cooking, seeks a commercial venue for her Ilocos empanadas, currently exploring food truck options.
Janz Kitchen plans to resume sales in May at MIXX360 nightclub as she fundraises for a food truck venture.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo paid high praise to Celtics’ coach Joe Mazzulla
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla for fostering a winning mentality despite team setbacks.
Joe Mazzulla guided the Celtics to second in the Eastern Conference amid key player absences and roster changes.
Milwaukee Bucks, led by Doc Rivers, missed playoffs for the first time in a decade with a 31-47 record.

title: Person seriously injured in fall at Firefighting Academy facility
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A person sustained serious injuries from a fall at the Firefighting Academy in Stow Tuesday morning.
Emergency crews responded at 9:05 a.m., providing medical care and requesting additional ambulance units.
Due to weather, ground transport was used to take the injured person to UMass Memorial Medical Center.

title: TravelMag went on a seafood road trip in Maine and named these ‘can’t-miss’ restaurants. Which is your favorite?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: TravelMag highlighted 10 'can't-miss' seafood restaurants in Maine during a coastal road trip.
Notable mentions include The Clam Shack in Kennebunkport and Bagaduce Lunch in Penobscot.
Readers are invited to share their favorite Maine seafood spots with Boston.com.

title: Ex-Stoughton cop Matthew Farwell’s DNA was on strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore, prosecutors say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matthew Farwell's DNA was found on the strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore, according to prosecutors' filings.
Farwell, a former Stoughton officer, allegedly killed Birchmore in 2021, believing he was the father of her unborn child.
Prosecutors argue Farwell's conduct indicated guilt, citing evidence of sexual exploitation and misleading investigators about his relationship with Birchmore.

title: A young mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into a Vermont river
author: John Waller
bullets: A 26-year-old mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into the Ottauquechee River in Vermont.
Hartford firefighters responded to the submerged vehicle report near Quechee Main Street shortly before 12:45 p.m. on Monday.
Both mother and child were evaluated at Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center and were found to have no serious injuries.

title: All the places you can go on the Amtrak Downeaster
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Amtrak Downeaster travels 143 miles from Boston's North Station to Brunswick, Maine, with five daily round-trips.
The train serves 12 stations in Massachusetts, New Hampshire, and Maine, offering amenities like Wi-Fi and a cafe.
Fares range from $3 to $38, with discounts for seniors and options for pets and bicycles with prior arrangement.

title: ‘Boston College is about to have a renaissance in basketball’: Dan Hurley’s take on Luke Murray
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Luke Murray, 41, will lead Boston College basketball after being hired in March to replace Earl Grant.
Dan Hurley praised Murray's impact at UConn, forecasting a basketball renaissance at Boston College under his leadership.
Murray, previously an assistant at UConn, helped the Huskies win two national championships since 2021.
